fields.name: Dallas to Atlanta FTL
fields.seo-title: Dallas, TX to Atlanta, GA FTL Freight | WARP
fields.seo-meta-description: Ship FTL freight from Dallas to Atlanta: 849-mile lane, 2–3 day transit, 27 carriers. Get instant rates and real-time tracking through WARP.
fields.hero-headline: Dallas, TX to Atlanta, GA FTL Freight
fields.subheadline: Dedicated capacity on a 849-mile corridor. GPS-equipped Dry Van 53', Flatbed 48', Reefer 53', 2–3 day transit, and live ETA from pickup to delivery. No terminal handoffs.
fields.body-content:
Dedicated FTL freight from Dallas, TX to Atlanta, GA: 849 miles, 2–3 day transit, point-to-point routing with no terminal handoffs. WARP covers this corridor with 27 vetted carriers providing Dry Van 53', Flatbed 48', Reefer 53' capacity. Every trailer is GPS-equipped with live ETA from dispatch through delivery.

Every FTL trailer on this corridor is GPS-equipped with live ETA updates. Geofenced arrival and departure alerts trigger automatically at origin and destination. WARP's operations team manages dispatch, driver coordination, and detention tracking. You get full shipment visibility without calling the carrier. If a load runs late or a driver misses a checkpoint, exception alerts fire within 30 minutes with automated escalation. Appointment scheduling and delivery confirmation are managed directly by WARP.

Cross-dock facilities at Dallas Mega Hub, Houston Terminal, San Antonio Relay support consolidation and re-routing on this corridor. When exceptions occur — weather delays, equipment failures, or volume surges — this infrastructure enables alternate routing without starting the shipment process over. Hurricane season Jun-Nov can disrupt Gulf lanes. Peak retail Oct-Dec.

FTL rates on this 849-mile corridor typically range from $1,868 to $2,225 depending on equipment type (dry van, flatbed, or reefer), spot-market capacity, and scheduling flexibility. At approximately $2.41/mile, the linehaul distance is the primary cost driver. Fuel surcharges are applied separately based on current diesel prices. Get a real-time rate at wearewarp.com/quote.

This corridor is a strong fit for full truckloads requiring dedicated transit, time-sensitive freight needing GPS tracking and live ETA, and shipments requiring specialized equipment. WARP is particularly useful when you need consistent Dallas to Atlanta capacity without maintaining individual carrier contracts or managing driver assignments directly.

Get an instant FTL rate for the Dallas to Atlanta lane at wearewarp.com/quote. Compare carrier options, book with a single click, and track every shipment with real-time milestone updates. No commitment required. See detailed operating data, pricing factors, and frequently asked questions for this corridor below.
fields.traditional-ltl:
Capacity Access: Multiple broker calls, no guarantee
GPS Tracking: Call carrier for location updates
Dispatch Speed: Hours to confirm driver assignment
Quote Speed: 2–24 hours via phone/email
Execution Control: Blind to driver ETA until arrival
Detention Tracking: Manual logs, disputed after the fact
Booking: Email chains with manual BOL creation
Exception Handling: Reactive, hours to discover
Pricing Transparency: Spot rates with hidden fees

Why the Dallas to Atlanta Corridor Matters

The Dallas to Atlanta FTL lane is a freight corridor connecting the South Central and Southeast regions. This 849-mile interstate lane connects two distinct freight markets, with transit driven by linehaul routing and terminal schedules. Standard FTL transit runs 2–3 business days. WARP operates this lane with 27 vetted carriers and common equipment including Dry Van 53', Flatbed 48', Reefer 53'. Cross-dock facilities at Dallas Mega Hub, Houston Terminal, San Antonio Relay support consolidation and transit optimization for this corridor. Hurricane season Jun-Nov can disrupt Gulf lanes. Peak retail Oct-Dec.

  • ▸ 849-mile corridor connecting South Central and Southeast regions
  • ▸ 2–3 business day standard transit for FTL service
  • ▸ 27 vetted carriers with Dry Van 53', Flatbed 48', Reefer 53' capacity
  • ▸ Cross-dock access at Dallas Mega Hub, Houston Terminal, San Antonio Relay
  • ▸ Hurricane season Jun-Nov can disrupt Gulf lanes. Peak retail Oct-Dec.

How WARP Operates the Dallas to Atlanta Lane

WARP covers the Dallas to Atlanta corridor with a vetted network of 27 carriers providing FTL capacity.

  • ✓ Dedicated trailers on this lane are GPS-equipped with live ETA updates and geofenced arrival/departure alerts.
  • ✓ You get full visibility without calling the carrier.

FTL Pricing Factors: Dallas to Atlanta

FTL rates on the Dallas to Atlanta lane typically range from $1,868 to $2,225, depending on freight class, weight, pallet count, and seasonal demand. Primary cost factors are lane distance, equipment type (dry van, flatbed, or reefer), and current spot-market capacity on this corridor. At 849 miles, the linehaul distance is the largest cost component (approximately $2.41/mile). Fuel surcharges are applied as a separate line item based on current diesel prices. These are modeled estimates. Enter your shipment details to get a real-time, all-in rate.

● Exception alerts within 30 minutes of status changes

Best-Fit Shipments: Dallas to Atlanta FTL

WARP is a strong fit for FTL shipments on the Dallas to Atlanta lane when you need dedicated capacity, GPS-equipped trailers, and managed operations.

  • ▸ Full truckload shipments requiring dedicated, non-stop transit on this corridor
  • ▸ Time-sensitive freight needing GPS tracking and live ETA updates
  • ▸ Shipments requiring specialized equipment (flatbed, reefer, or dry van)
  • ▸ Shippers who need capacity without maintaining individual carrier contracts
  • ▸ Freight operations that require automated exception detection and escalation

Why Shippers Use WARP for Dallas to Atlanta Freight

Self-Serve Instant Quoting

Get FTL rates for the Dallas to Atlanta lane in under 2 minutes. No phone calls, no email chains, no waiting for a broker to call back. Enter your freight details and compare carrier options side-by-side.

Real-Time Shipment Visibility

Every FTL shipment on this 849-mile corridor includes milestone tracking from pickup through delivery. Exception alerts fire within 30 minutes of any status change — you see issues before they become problems.

Managed Operations

WARP's operations team handles carrier coordination, appointment scheduling, and exception resolution on the Dallas to Atlanta lane. You move freight without managing carrier relationships.

Vetted Carrier Network

27 vetted carriers operate this corridor, providing consistent FTL capacity. Carrier selection is optimized per-shipment based on performance history and equipment fit.

Cross-Dock Infrastructure

Cross-dock facilities at Dallas Mega Hub and Houston Terminal support consolidation and re-routing when exceptions occur on this corridor. This infrastructure enables better transit reliability on the Dallas to Atlanta lane.

Transparent Pricing

All-in FTL rates with no hidden fees. Fuel surcharges, accessorials, and linehaul are broken out clearly. See exactly what you're paying before you book.

Frequently Asked Questions: Dallas to Atlanta FTL

How much does FTL freight from Dallas, TX to Atlanta, GA cost?

Estimated FTL rates on this lane range from $1,868 to $2,225, based on freight class, weight, pallet count, and current market conditions. Enter your shipment details on WARP for an instant, all-in rate.

What is the transit time for FTL shipments from Dallas to Atlanta?

Standard FTL transit on the Dallas to Atlanta lane is 2–3 business days. This 849-mile corridor's actual transit depends on carrier routing, terminal schedules, and pickup timing.

What carriers does WARP use on the Dallas to Atlanta lane?

WARP operates this lane with approximately 27 vetted carriers. Carrier selection is optimized for each shipment based on equipment requirements, transit targets, and on-time performance history.

Can I track my FTL shipment from Dallas to Atlanta in real time?

Yes. Every WARP shipment includes real-time tracking with scan events at pickup, in-transit checkpoints, and delivery. Exception alerts fire within 30 minutes of any status change, and WARP's operations team manages resolution directly without requiring shipper follow-up.

What equipment is available for FTL freight on this lane?

Common equipment on the Dallas to Atlanta corridor includes Dry Van 53', Flatbed 48', Reefer 53'. Equipment availability varies by season and demand. Specify your requirements when requesting a quote.

What affects FTL freight rates from Dallas to Atlanta?

Key rate factors on this lane include freight class (NMFC classification), shipment weight and density, pallet count, accessorial services (liftgate, inside delivery, limited access), seasonal demand, and current carrier capacity. Fuel surcharges are applied separately based on current diesel prices.

What makes WARP different from traditional FTL freight brokers?

WARP provides self-serve instant quoting (under 2 minutes vs. hours with traditional brokers), a side-by-side carrier comparison dashboard, one-click booking with digital BOL, real-time tracking with proactive exception alerts, and managed operations where WARP handles carrier coordination and resolution directly.
